task_completion.description: Open the air.mon.ltm.nc dataset in NASA Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application is open, and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the Sources/Datasets list.

task_completion.description: Create a geo-mapped plot for the 'air' variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot window is open in Panoply displaying a map, with the variable 'air' (Long Term Mean Air Temperature) selected as the data source.

task_completion.description: Set the time step to July
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Panoply plot window (typically under the 'Array' tab or in the plot title/subtitle), the time dimension is set to 'July' or the corresponding index for July.

task_completion.description: Apply a Robinson projection centered on 180° Longitude
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map visually shows a Robinson projection (flattened poles, elliptical sides) centered on the Pacific Ocean. In the 'Map' tab of the plot window, the Projection is set to 'Robinson' and Center Lon is set to '180'.

task_completion.description: Apply a perceptually uniform color scale
task_completion.visual_evidence: The plot uses a perceptually uniform color scale (e.g., Viridis, Cividis, Plasma, Magma, or Inferno) instead of the default rainbow scale. This can be verified visually on the map's color bar or in the 'Scale' tab settings.

task_completion.description: Export the visualization PNG and metadata text file to the IPCC_Report directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor shows 'air_temp_july_accessible.png' and 'visualization_metadata.txt' successfully saved in the '~/Documents/IPCC_Report' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Panoply GUI to configure the plot
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with Panoply's interface (clicking tabs like Map, Scale, Array, and changing dropdowns) rather than bypassing the GUI with an external plotting script.

integrity.description: Verify the exported image matches the Panoply configuration
integrity.visual_evidence: The final exported 'air_temp_july_accessible.png' should visually match the plot configured in the Panoply GUI window, proving it was exported from the application.

integrity.description: Verify the metadata file was genuinely created and populated
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen opening a text editor or using terminal commands to write the required metadata fields into 'visualization_metadata.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using NASA Panoply to open '/home/ga/PanoplyData/air.mon.ltm.nc' and plot the 'air' variable. The plot must be configured with a 'Robinson' projection, centered at '180' degrees longitude, and use a perceptually uniform color scale (viridis, cividis, plasma, magma, or inferno). The final outputs must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/IPCC_Report/' as 'air_temp_july_accessible.png' and 'visualization_metadata.txt'.
